title: ORTHO GP FOLLOW-UP
context: After seeing Ramaiah (68M, cardiac stent, chronic knee pain) — 9th visit
transcript:
Ramaiah uncle vacharu, same story, molli noppi tho bothered avthunnaru, Ibuprofen
kavali ani aduguthunnaru malli, ninth time aduguthunnaru, stent undi kadha ivvakudadu
ani cheppanu malli, family ki kuda cheppanu — koduku kuda aduguthadu phone chesi —
Paracetamol 650 QDS continue, Tramadol 50 add chesthunna ippudu VAS 7 undi, topical
Diclofenac gel okay kaani oral NSAIDs enduku ivvakudado malli explain chesanu,
next week ki rammani cheppanu, aa lopu dizziness vosthe call cheyamani cheppanu
Tramadol valla, and Priya sister ki cheppandi vitals record cheyamani.
translation: Ramaiah uncle came, same complaint, bothered by knee pain, asking for Ibuprofen AGAIN (9th time), told him can't give due to stent, also told family — son also calls asking — Paracetamol 650 QDS continue, adding Tramadol 50 now because VAS is 7, topical Diclofenac gel is okay but explained again why no oral NSAIDs, told him to come next week, if dizziness from Tramadol then call, and tell Nurse Priya to record vitals.

expected_nodes.content: Ramaiah — NO oral NSAIDs (9th refusal documented, stent, family also requesting)
Tramadol 50mg added (VAS 7, escalation from Paracetamol alone)
Family behavior — son calls requesting Ibuprofen, needs continued education
Follow-up next week, monitor dizziness (Tramadol side effect)

why_fails: "ivvakudadu" (must not give) appears TWICE — negation critical both times
"ninth time aduguthunnaru" — behavioral pattern tracking, not just a number
"oral NSAIDs enduku ivvakudado" — the reason WHY not to give, in Telugu
"Priya sister ki cheppandi" — instruction to nurse, not a clinical decision
The entire note has ONE continuous thought stream with no punctuation

title: MEDICINE WARD ROUND
context: Dictating about 3 patients during morning rounds — rapid switching
transcript:
Bed 14 Sharma ji — sugar fasting 280 hai post-prandial 380, Metformin 1000 BD chal
raha hai lekin kaam nahi kar raha, Glimepiride 2 se 4 karo, nahi nahi wait karo pehle
HbA1c dekho result aaya kya pharmacy se, agar 9 se zyada hai to seedha insulin start
karna padega, Lantus 10 units raat ko — okay next — bed 16 Gupta aunty — BP aaj
theek hai 130 by 80, Telmisartan 40 continue, potassium low tha kal 3.2 tha aaj ka
result aaya kya check karo, Dytor se potassium girta hai — okay next — bed 18
Yadav sahab — discharge karo aaj, medicines likho — Tab Ecosprin 75 OD, Tab Atorva
40 HS, Tab Metolar 50 BD, Tab Pan 40 OD empty stomach, follow-up 2 hafte mein,
agar chest pain aaye to seedha emergency aaye mat ghar pe baitho.
translation: Three patients in rapid sequence during ward rounds: Sharma (sugar uncontrolled, considering insulin), Gupta (BP stable, potassium monitoring needed), Yadav (discharge with 4-drug prescription and emergency instructions).

expected_nodes.content: Sharma — Glimepiride 2→4mg pending HbA1c; if HbA1c > 9 → Lantus 10u HS
Gupta — BP 130/80 stable on Telmisartan 40, potassium was 3.2 (low, Dytor side effect)
Yadav discharge — Ecosprin 75, Atorvastatin 40, Metoprolol 50 BD, Pantoprazole 40
Yadav — if chest pain, come to emergency IMMEDIATELY, don't wait at home

why_fails: Self-correction mid-sentence: "Glimepiride 2 se 4 karo, nahi nahi wait karo" — doctor changes mind
Three patients in ONE voice note — must separate context
"Ecosprin" "Atorva" "Metolar" "Pan" — all Indian brand names
"mat ghar pe baitho" (don't sit at home) — negation in colloquial Hindi
"Dytor se potassium girta hai" — clinical reasoning about drug side effect

title: PAEDIATRICS
context: After seeing Aadhya (3.5F, penicillin allergy, recurrent ear infections) — mother is anxious
transcript:
Aadhya baby ku ippo fifth time ear infection, amma romba worried — amoxicillin
kudukka kudadhu allergy iruku 18 months la anaphylaxis vanthuchu amma ku explain
panninom but amma every time kekuranga amoxicillin than work aagum nu —
azithromycin 10mg per kg 3 days start pannanum, weight 14kg so total 140mg per
day, oru 5ml spoon la suspend pannunga, ENT referral write pannunga Dr. Krishnan
ku — ippadi frequent ah varthu na adenoid check pannanum, tympanometry pannanum,
hearing test kuda pannanum — amma kitta sollunga next week appointment fix
panniduvom ENT ku, aana ippo azithromycin start pannidalam paravaillai.
translation: Aadhya's 5th ear infection, mother is very worried, keeps asking for amoxicillin despite anaphylaxis history, prescribed azithromycin weight-based (14kg × 10mg = 140mg), ENT referral to Dr. Krishnan needed — if this frequent need to check adenoids, tympanometry, hearing test. Start azithromycin now, ENT appointment next week.

expected_nodes.content: Aadhya — NO amoxicillin/penicillin (anaphylaxis at 18 months, mother repeatedly requests)
Azithromycin 140mg/day (10mg/kg × 14kg) × 3 days
ENT referral to Dr. Krishnan — adenoid check, tympanometry, hearing test
Mother behavior — requests amoxicillin every visit despite allergy education
5th ear infection — frequency pattern warrants ENT evaluation

why_fails: "kudukka kudadhu" (must not give) — Tamil double negative, critical for patient safety
Weight-based calculation embedded in speech: "10mg per kg...weight 14kg so 140mg"
"amma every time kekuranga" — behavioral pattern in Tamil
"paravaillai" (it's okay) at the end — contextual approval to start, not a medical term
Clinical reasoning chain: frequent infections → adenoid suspicion → ENT referral

title: CARDIOLOGY CCU
context: After emergency admission — Mr. Rajan's chest pain episode
transcript:
Rajan garu CCU lo admit ayyaru chest pain tho, ECG lo ST elevation V1 to V4
anterior wall MI, troponin send chesamu stat result kosam wait chesthunnam,
Cath lab ni alert chesamu — kaani problem emitante Rajan gari ki already Warfarin
chal thundi INR 2.8 undi, dual antiplatelet start cheyali kaani bleeding risk
ekkuva, so Heparin bolus ivvandi 60 units per kg but max 4000 units, Clopidogrel
300mg loading dose ivvandi — KAANI Aspirin loading dose ivvakandi INR ekkuva
undi kadha Warfarin valla — cardiology fellow ki cheppandi Dr. Ravi ki — ayana
cath lab lo wait chesthadu — family ki cheppandi serious condition ani consent
form sign cheyinchandi cath ki — oxygen 4 litre nasal prongs, Morphine 2mg IV
pain kosam ichi already — vitals every 15 minutes.
translation: Rajan admitted to CCU with chest pain, ECG shows anterior STEMI (V1-V4), troponin sent stat, cath lab alerted. KEY DILEMMA: already on Warfarin with INR 2.8, needs dual antiplatelet but high bleeding risk. Give Heparin 60u/kg (max 4000u), Clopidogrel 300mg loading — BUT DON'T give Aspirin loading (INR too high on Warfarin). Call cardiology fellow Dr. Ravi, tell family it's serious, get cath consent, oxygen 4L, already gave Morphine 2mg IV.

expected_nodes.content: Rajan — anterior STEMI (ST elevation V1-V4), admitted CCU
DO NOT give Aspirin loading — INR 2.8 on Warfarin, bleeding risk
Heparin 60u/kg (max 4000u) + Clopidogrel 300mg loading
Morphine 2mg IV given for pain, O2 4L nasal prongs
Cath lab activation, Dr. Ravi (fellow) informed

why_fails: "KAANI Aspirin loading dose ivvakandi" — "KAANI" (BUT) signals a CRITICAL exception, "ivvakandi" = DON'T give
Complex clinical reasoning: Warfarin + STEMI → can't use standard dual antiplatelet protocol
"60 units per kg but max 4000 units" — conditional dosing with ceiling
Multiple simultaneous instructions to different people (nurse, fellow, family)
"INR ekkuva undi kadha Warfarin valla" — the REASON embedded in Telugu

title: GP CLINIC
context: Frustrated with non-compliant diabetic patient — Padma aunty (62F, fasting)
transcript:
Padma aunty ka case bahut frustrating hai — sugar 380 aa raha hai fasting, maine
bola tha Ekadashi ko Glimepiride skip karo lekin aunty ne suna nahi, dono tablet
kha li khali pet, hypoglycemia ho gaya ghar pe, bete ne phone kiya ki aunty behosh
ho gayi — thankfully glucose de diya — ab main kya karun — Glimepiride 2mg se 1mg
karo pehle, Metformin 1000 continue, HbA1c karwao, agar 8 se zyada hai to Insulin
pe shift karna padega — Padma aunty ke bete ko bolo ki Ekadashi ke din SIRF
Metformin dena, Glimepiride bilkul nahi, aur fast break karne ka time fix karo
sham 6 baje, usse pehle BG check karo har 4 ghante — aur haan dietician referral
do aunty ko — low glycemic index wala diet chahiye fasting ke liye.
translation: Frustrated: Padma's sugar 380 fasting, told her to skip Glimepiride on Ekadashi but she didn't listen, took both tablets on empty stomach, had hypoglycemia at home, son called saying she fainted. Reducing Glimepiride 2mg→1mg, Metformin continue, HbA1c needed — if >8 switch to insulin. Tell son: ONLY Metformin on Ekadashi, NO Glimepiride, fix fast-break time at 6pm, BG every 4 hours. Dietician referral for low-GI fasting diet.

expected_nodes.content: Padma took Glimepiride on fasting day despite instructions → hypoglycemia episode
Reduce Glimepiride 2mg→1mg, conditional insulin if HbA1c > 8
Ekadashi days — ONLY Metformin, NO Glimepiride (patient AND son must be told)
Fixed fast-break time 6pm, BG monitoring q4h on fast days
Dietician referral for low-GI fasting diet
Hypoglycemia event — patient fainted at home, son administered glucose

why_fails: "aunty ne suna nahi" (didn't listen) — non-compliance narrative in Hindi
"dono tablet kha li khali pet" — took BOTH tablets on empty stomach (clinical error by patient)
"Glimepiride bilkul nahi" — emphatic negation "absolutely not"
"haan dietician referral do" — "haan" (yes) is self-talk, not clinical
The FRUSTRATION in the doctor's tone changes speech pattern — more Hindi, less English

title: SURGERY PRE-OP
context: Planning for Ramaiah's TKR surgery — complex medication management
transcript:
Ramaiah avrige TKR surgery plan maadthiddivi next week, problem enu andre
Warfarin chal aagidhe, INR 2.6 idhe, surgery ge 5 dina munche Warfarin
nillisbekku, Heparin bridging start maadbekku Enoxaparin 60mg BD, INR 1.5 ge
barbekku surgery munche — Cardiology team Dr. Mehta ge discuss maadbekku because
stent idhe 2022 nalli — Clopidogrel 7 days munche nillisbekku but Aspirin
continue maadona last minute vargu — anaesthesia team ge helbekku difficult
airway iddare, pre-op blood work CBC, RFT, LFT, coagulation profile, chest
X-ray, ECG — physiotherapy team ge inform maadi post-op day 1 ne PT start
aagbekku — Priya sister ge helu special attention Warfarin restart timing
post-op Dr. Mehta decide maadthare.
translation: Planning Ramaiah's TKR next week. Problem: on Warfarin (INR 2.6) — stop 5 days before, bridge with Enoxaparin 60mg BD, target INR 1.5 pre-op. Discuss with Cardiology (Dr. Mehta) because of stent (2022). Stop Clopidogrel 7 days before but continue Aspirin until last moment. Inform anaesthesia (possible difficult airway). Pre-op workup: CBC, RFT, LFT, coagulation, CXR, ECG. PT team: start Day 1 post-op. Nurse Priya: special attention to Warfarin restart timing — Dr. Mehta decides.

expected_nodes.content: TKR next week — Warfarin stop 5 days pre-op, Enoxaparin 60mg BD bridging
Stop Clopidogrel 7 days pre-op, continue Aspirin until last moment
Must discuss with Cardiology (Dr. Mehta) — stent 2022
Pre-op workup list (CBC, RFT, LFT, coagulation, CXR, ECG)
PT start Day 1 post-op
Warfarin restart timing decided by Cardiology, not surgery

why_fails: "nillisbekku" (must stop) — different from "nillisi" (stop) — urgency in Kannada
Multiple teams coordinated in one note (surgery, cardiology, anaesthesia, PT, nursing)
"last minute vargu" (until last moment) — colloquial Kannada-English timing
"Dr. Mehta decide maadthare" — attribution of decision authority to another doctor
Complex perioperative anticoagulation reasoning in code-mixed speech

title: PSYCHIATRY
context: After seeing a depressed patient — Arun (45M, first episode, reluctant about medication)
transcript:
Arun babu ashechilen aaj, PHQ-9 score 18 porjonto bere gechhe, moderate to severe
depression, ghum nei rate — 3-4 ghonta matro, khabar khacche na thik moto — 5kg
weight kome gechhe last 2 months e — Nexito 10mg raat e start korte hobe, kaaj na
korle 2 week pore 20mg te barhabo — Clonazepam 0.5mg raat e initially neend er jonno
kaaron Nexito te first 2 weeks e insomnia barte pare — important kotha — Arun babu
antidepressant nite raaji non, bolchhen 'pagol er oshudh' — bolechhi je ei oshudh
diabetes er oshudh er moto, brain er chemical balance correct kore — tai ke bolo
next visit e ashe je counseling o shuru korbo — suicidal ideation nei ekhon but
safety plan discuss korechhi, wife er number niye rekhechhi emergency te — follow-up
1 week e.
translation: Arun came today, PHQ-9 risen to 18 (moderate-severe), not sleeping (3-4 hours only), not eating properly — lost 5kg in 2 months. Starting Escitalopram 10mg at night, increase to 20mg in 2 weeks if not working. Clonazepam 0.5mg at night initially for sleep because Escitalopram may worsen insomnia first 2 weeks. IMPORTANT: Arun refuses antidepressant — calls it "pagol er oshudh" (madman's medicine). Explained it's like diabetes medicine — corrects brain chemical balance. Told wife to bring him next visit for counseling. No suicidal ideation currently but discussed safety plan, took wife's emergency number. Follow-up 1 week.

expected_nodes.content: Arun — PHQ-9: 18, insomnia (3-4hr), anorexia, 5kg weight loss in 2 months
Start Escitalopram 10mg HS → increase to 20mg if no response in 2 weeks
Clonazepam 0.5mg HS temporarily (Escitalopram initial insomnia risk)
Patient stigma — calls antidepressant "pagol er oshudh" (madman's medicine)
No suicidal ideation currently, safety plan discussed, wife's emergency number obtained
Counseling to start next visit, wife to accompany

why_fails: "pagol er oshudh" — Bengali slang for psychiatric medication stigma, not translatable
"Nexito" → brand name for Escitalopram, ChatGPT sees "next to"
The therapeutic reasoning: WHY Clonazepam added (initial insomnia from SSRI) — embedded in Bengali
"raaji non" (not agreeing) — negation in Bengali, patient's refusal
Safety assessment embedded naturally — not structured

title: OB-GYN
context: After prenatal visit — high-risk pregnancy with pre-eclampsia risk
transcript:
Meera chechi 32 weeks aayallo, BP innale 148 by 92 aayirunnu innu 152 by 96
aanu, urine protein 2+ aanu — pre-eclampsia concern undu — Labetalol 100mg BD
start cheyyuka, ACE inhibitor paadilla pregnancy yil absolutely contraindicated
aanu, Methyldopa aanu alternative — but Labetalol try cheyyam aadyam —
24-hour urine protein collection start cheyyamaanu, complete blood count
platelets eppol check cheyyuka, LDH uric acid liver enzymes ellaam cheyyuka
HELLP rule out cheyyaan — baby yude growth scan next week, CTG biweekly
start cheyyuka — Meera chechi yodu paranju bed rest suggest cheyyu but
complete bed rest venda moderate activity okay aanu — eppozhaanu hospital
il varanam enu paranjittundu — headache visual disturbance epigastric pain
ingane enthenkilum undenkil udan varanam — husband odu koodhi paranju
emergency signs — next visit 3 days kazhinju.
translation: Meera at 32 weeks, BP rising (148/92 → 152/96), urine protein 2+ — pre-eclampsia concern. Start Labetalol 100mg BD. ACE inhibitor ABSOLUTELY contraindicated in pregnancy. Methyldopa is alternative but trying Labetalol first. 24-hr urine protein collection, CBC with platelets NOW, LDH, uric acid, liver enzymes — rule out HELLP. Growth scan next week, biweekly CTG. Told Meera: moderate bed rest (not complete), come to hospital if headache, visual disturbance, or epigastric pain. Explained emergency signs to husband also. Follow-up in 3 days.

expected_nodes.content: ACE inhibitor ABSOLUTELY contraindicated in pregnancy
Labetalol 100mg BD started for pre-eclampsia (alternative: Methyldopa)
HELLP workup: 24hr urine protein, CBC+platelets, LDH, uric acid, LFT
Growth scan next week, biweekly CTG
Emergency signs — headache, visual disturbance, epigastric pain → immediate hospital
Meera 32 weeks — BP 152/96, urine protein 2+, pre-eclampsia suspected

why_fails: "paadilla" (must not) → Malayalam negation lost → could generate "give ACE inhibitor"
"cheyyamaanu" (should be done) → complex Malayalam verb → garbled
"venda" (don't want/need) — "complete bed rest venda" → negation for bed rest
"ingane enthenkilum undenkil udan varanam" — conditional emergency instruction in pure Malayalam
Multiple care plans in one note (maternal, fetal, labs, follow-up)

title: EMERGENCY MEDICINE
context: Active resuscitation — speaking FAST, giving orders
transcript:
Multiple trauma hai RTA sixteen year old ladka — GCS 9 hai E2V3M4 —
intubation karo size 7 tube — C-spine collar lagao pehle — BP 80 by 50 hai
NS bolus do 20ml per kg stat means 1 litre do — type and cross match karo
4 units PRBC — ek haath mein wide bore cannula daalo 16 gauge — doosre
haath mein bhi ek aur — Tetanus TT do kya history hai check karo — chest
X-ray portable karo pneumothorax rule out karo left side pe breath sounds
kam hain — CT head stat karo — neuro ko bulao Dr. Verma ko — ortho ko bhi
bulao right femur deformed lag raha hai — parents ko bulao consent chahiye —
agar BP respond nahi karta NS ke baad to blood shuru karo — Tranexamic
acid 1 gram over 10 min do — urine catheter daalo urine output monitor
karo — FAST ultrasound karo free fluid check karo!
translation: Multiple trauma, 16-year-old boy, RTA. GCS 9 (E2V3M4). Intubate with size 7 tube — C-spine collar FIRST. BP 80/50 — NS bolus 20ml/kg = 1 litre stat. Type and crossmatch 4 units PRBC. Two wide bore cannulas 16G. Check tetanus history. Portable CXR — rule out pneumothorax (decreased breath sounds left). Stat CT head. Call Neuro (Dr. Verma) and Ortho (right femur deformity). Call parents for consent. If BP doesn't respond to NS → start blood. Tranexamic acid 1g over 10 min. Foley catheter, monitor urine output. FAST scan for free fluid.

expected_nodes.content: 16M, RTA, GCS 9 (E2V3M4), BP 80/50, left decreased breath sounds, right femur deformity
Intubate 7.0 ET, C-spine collar first, bilateral 16G IV access
NS 20ml/kg (1L) stat → if no response → blood transfusion
Tranexamic acid 1g/10min, 4 units PRBC crossmatched
CT head stat, CXR portable (pneumothorax r/o), FAST scan
Neuro (Dr. Verma) + Ortho consults called

why_fails: Extremely rapid speech with no pauses or punctuation
"C-spine collar lagao pehle" — order of operations critical (collar BEFORE intubation)
"agar BP respond nahi karta...to blood shuru karo" — conditional in rapid Hindi
Multiple simultaneous orders to different team members
"16 gauge" "size 7" "20ml per kg" — numbers embedded in rapid stream

title: DERMATOLOGY
context: After seeing a child with severe eczema — mother using wrong treatment
transcript:
Arjun baby ne eczema vadhare kharab thayo che — mummy homeopathy ni
goli aapi rahi che sathe sathe, mane khabar nahi kem jaldi nathi thatu —
maine dekha ki ek steroid cream lagavi che jeni koi prescription nathi —
medical store wale ne aa cream aapi — bahut strong Clobetasol 0.05% face
pe lagavi che! — bachche ke face pe Class 1 steroid nahi lagana — skin
atrophy thay jase — abhi Mometasone 0.1% shift karo — face pe sirf 5 din,
body pe 10 din — moisturizer din ma chaar vaar lagavo — Cetaphil ya
Physiogel — emollient pehle fir steroid upar se — bathing routine change
karo lukewarm water sirf 5 minute — mummy ne samjhavo ki homeopathy band
karo jab tak allopathy chal rahi hai interaction ho sakta hai — aur haan
medical store se direct steroid lena band karo dangerous hai — follow-up
2 weeks.
translation: Arjun's eczema worsening — mother giving homeopathy simultaneously AND applying unprescribed Clobetasol 0.05% (class 1 steroid!) on FACE — bought from medical store without prescription. NEVER use Class 1 steroid on child's face — risk of skin atrophy. Switch to Mometasone 0.1% — face 5 days max, body 10 days. Moisturizer QID (Cetaphil/Physiogel) — emollient first then steroid. Change bathing to lukewarm 5 minutes only. Tell mother: stop homeopathy while on allopathy (interaction risk) and stop buying steroids from store directly — it's dangerous. Follow-up 2 weeks.

expected_nodes.content: Mother applying Class 1 steroid (Clobetasol) on child's face without prescription — DANGEROUS
NEVER Class 1 steroid on paediatric face — skin atrophy risk
Switch to Mometasone 0.1% (face 5 days, body 10 days)
Moisturizer QID (Cetaphil/Physiogel) — emollient before steroid
Mother using homeopathy + allopathy simultaneously — interaction risk
Buying prescription steroids OTC from medical store

why_fails: Triple code-switch: Gujarati + Hindi + English in one sentence
"jeni koi prescription nathi" (no prescription for it) — Gujarati negation
Brand names: Clobetasol, Mometasone, Cetaphil, Physiogel — mixed with Gujarati
Social context (homeopathy use, OTC steroid purchase) — critical for patient education
"emollient pehle fir steroid upar se" — application ORDER matters clinically

title: NEPHROLOGY
context: CKD patient with medication adjustment needed
transcript:
Lakshman gari creatinine 3.8 ki vachindi, GFR calculate chesthe 18 vachindi,
CKD stage 4 ki progress ayyindi — Metformin ippatiki ippatiki aapeyandi
contraindicated below GFR 30 lo — Linagliptin ki shift cheyandi kidney safe
adi — Ramipril dose 10 nunchi 5mg ki tagginchandi proteinuria thagguthundhaa
ani chuddham — potassium ekkuva unnadi 5.8 — Kayexalate ivvandi stat aur
calcium gluconate ready ga pettandi ECG changes unte — eppudu Torsemide
20mg aapeyandi Furosemide 40mg ki maaripoyandi — aur haan nephrology OPD
ki next week rammani cheppandi dialysis planning discussion cheyali —
dietician ki refer cheyandi low protein low potassium low sodium diet.

expected_nodes.content: Lakshman — Creatinine 3.8, GFR 18, progressed to CKD Stage 4
Stop Metformin — contraindicated below GFR 30
Switch to Linagliptin (kidney safe)
Reduce Ramipril 10→5mg, monitor proteinuria
Potassium 5.8 (high) — Kayexalate stat, Calcium gluconate ready for ECG changes
Switch Torsemide 20→Furosemide 40, nephrology referral for dialysis planning

title: ENT
context: After seeing a patient with sudden hearing loss — time-sensitive treatment
transcript:
Deshmukh sahab yeun gele, sudden sensorineural hearing loss ahe right side
la — onset 2 divas aadhi, morning uthlyavar suddenly aikku yet navhta —
pure tone audiometry karun ghya urgently — ata Prednisolone 1mg per kg
start kara means 60mg morning 2 weeks tapering dose — intratympanic
Dexamethasone consider karun ya — MRI brain karun ghya acoustic neuroma
rule out karayala — patient la sanga 48 hours madhye treatment start nahi
kela tar recovery chances kammi hotat — Betahistine 16mg TDS suru kara
vertigo asel tar — audiometry result baghun tya nusaar pudhe plan karu —
smoking band kara lau immediately circulation improve vhayala.

expected_nodes.content: Deshmukh — sudden sensorineural hearing loss (right), onset 2 days ago
Treatment must start within 48 hours or recovery chances decrease
Prednisolone 60mg (1mg/kg) × 2 weeks taper, consider intratympanic Dexamethasone
MRI brain to rule out acoustic neuroma
Betahistine 16mg TDS if vertigo present
Stop smoking immediately — improve circulation

title: PULMONOLOGY
context: COPD exacerbation with home oxygen misuse
transcript:
Tripathi ji ka COPD exacerbation hai — saturation 82% pe aaye the — problem
ye hai ki ghar pe oxygen concentrator chal raha hai lekin 8 litre pe rakh
rakha hai — CO2 narcosis ka risk hai bhai sahab — 2 litre pe set karo target
88 to 92 saturation rakhna hai zyada nahi — Nebulization karo Duolin plus
Budecort, steroids do Methylprednisolone 40mg IV BD 5 din ke liye —
antibiotics chahiye sputum purulent hai Amoxiclav 1.2g IV BD start karo —
ABG karo CO2 dekho agar 60 se zyada hai to BiPAP lagao — chest X-ray karo
consolidation hai ya nahi — Tripathi ji ko samjhao ki oxygen zyada dene se
brain ko signal jaata hai ki breathe mat karo — ye life threatening hai —
family ko bhi samjhao ghar pe 2 litre se zyada nahi dena kabhi bhi.

expected_nodes.content: Patient using home O2 at 8L — CO2 narcosis risk (should be 2L, target SpO2 88-92%)
Nebulization (Duolin + Budecort), Methylprednisolone 40mg IV BD × 5 days
Amoxiclav 1.2g IV BD (purulent sputum)
ABG — if CO2 > 60 → BiPAP
Home O2 NEVER > 2L — explain to patient AND family (life threatening)
SpO2 82% on arrival, COPD exacerbation

title: ONCOLOGY
context: Breaking bad news + treatment planning — emotionally heavy
transcript:
Biopsy result vanthuchu — Kamala paatti ku Stage 3 breast cancer confirm
aayiduchu — ennoda colleague Dr. Raman oncology la discuss panninom —
neoadjuvant chemotherapy start pannanum TC regimen — Paclitaxel 175mg per
m2 plus Carboplatin AUC 5 — 6 cycles 3 weeks interval — port placement
pannanum chemotherapy ku before — GCSF support venum nadir la neutropenia
varum — anti-emetic ondansetron plus dexamethasone pre-chemo — echocardiography
pannanum baseline cardiac function paarkka Paclitaxel cardiotoxicity risk iruku —
Kamala paatti kitta eppadi sollrathu — family meeting arrange pannunga —
daughter Meera doctor aa irukanga Chennai la — avaanga kitta munnadi pesum —
psychological support referral pannunga — oru vishayam — Kamala paatti
diabetes iruku Metformin chal aagudhu — chemo time la sugar control romba
important steroids sugar eththum.

expected_nodes.content: Kamala — Stage 3 breast cancer confirmed (biopsy)
Neoadjuvant TC regimen: Paclitaxel 175mg/m² + Carboplatin AUC 5, 6 cycles q3w
Port placement before chemo, GCSF for nadir neutropenia
Pre-chemo antiemetics: Ondansetron + Dexamethasone
Baseline echo (Paclitaxel cardiotoxicity risk)
Diabetes management critical during chemo — steroids will raise sugar
Family meeting — contact daughter Meera (doctor, Chennai) first, psych support referral

title: GASTROENTEROLOGY
context: Liver cirrhosis patient with ascites — complex management
transcript:
Subrahmanyam gari ki liver cirrhosis advance ayyindi — ascites perigu thunnadi
albumin 2.1 ki thaggindi — large volume paracentesis cheyali 5 litres drain
chesthamu — prathi litre ki 8 grams albumin replace cheyali means 40 grams
albumin ivvali — Spironolactone 100mg start cheyandi Furosemide 40mg kuda —
kaani potassium check cheyandi Spironolactone potassium peruguthundi — sodium
restrict cheyandi 2 grams per day — SBP prophylaxis kosam Norfloxacin 400mg
OD start cheyandi ascitic fluid analysis send chesamu cell count albumin
protein culture — UGI endoscopy schedule cheyandi varices check kosam —
Lactulose 30ml TDS start cheyandi ammonia control kosam hepatic encephalopathy
prevention ki — aur bhai Rifaximin 550 BD add cheyandi kuda — liver transplant
evaluation ki referral raasinchandi Hyderabad KIMS ki — MELD score calculate
cheyandi.

expected_nodes.content: Subrahmanyam — advanced cirrhosis, ascites, albumin 2.1
Large volume paracentesis (5L) with albumin replacement (8g/L = 40g total)
Spironolactone 100mg + Furosemide 40mg (diuretics), sodium restrict 2g/day
SBP prophylaxis — Norfloxacin 400mg OD, ascitic fluid analysis sent
UGI endoscopy for varices, Lactulose 30ml TDS + Rifaximin 550 BD (encephalopathy prevention)
Liver transplant evaluation referral to KIMS Hyderabad, calculate MELD score

title: ORTHO POST-OP
context: Junior dictating post-op note on behalf of HOD — nervous, careful
transcript:
Dr. Vikram sir ke instruction ke hisaab se — Ramaiah uncle ka TKR hua hai
right knee — Zimmer implant use hua hai size 5 — surgery time 1 hour 45
minute — blood loss approximately 350ml — drain rakhna hai 48 hours —
CPM machine day 2 se start — physiotherapy day 1 se — ankle pumps aur
static quads — weight bearing walker ke saath day 2 se — pain management
Paracetamol 1g IV 6 hourly first 24 hours phir oral 650mg QDS — Tramadol
50mg IV SOS agar VAS 6 se zyada — DVT prophylaxis Enoxaparin 40mg SC
subah se — 12 hours baad surgery ke — 14 din tak — antibiotics Cefuroxime
1.5g IV BD 48 hours phir oral Cefuroxime 500 BD 5 din — Warfarin RESTART
karna hai but Dr. Mehta cardiology se discuss karke — khud se shuru mat
karna — INR monitoring start karna hai day 3 se.

expected_nodes.content: Ramaiah — right TKR done, Zimmer size 5, 1hr 45min, blood loss 350ml
Pain — Paracetamol 1g IV q6h (24hr) → oral 650mg QDS, Tramadol 50mg IV SOS if VAS > 6
DVT — Enoxaparin 40mg SC daily from 12hr post-op × 14 days
Antibiotics — Cefuroxime 1.5g IV BD (48hr) → oral 500mg BD (5 days)
PT Day 1 (ankle pumps, static quads), CPM Day 2, weight-bearing walker Day 2
Warfarin restart ONLY after Cardiology (Dr. Mehta) discussion — do NOT start independently
Drain 48 hours, INR monitoring from Day 3

title: ENDOCRINOLOGY
context: Thyroid management with pregnancy planning
transcript:
Nisha chechi thyroid case aanu — TSH 12.5 aanu hypothyroid confirm aanu —
Thyronorm 75mcg raavile kazhikkanam vayattil — 30 minutes kazhinjittu mathram
breakfast kazhikkuka — important vishayam — Nisha chechi pregnancy plan
cheyunnundu 3 months kazhinju — TSH 2.5 il thaazhe kondu varanam pregnancy
munpu — 4 weeks il TSH repeat cheyyuka — dose increase vendum aayirikkum
100mcg vareyum — calcium iron supplements Thyronorm inte koode kazhikkaan
paadilla 4 hour gap venam — ippo Calcirol aanu kazhikkunnathu — athu
vaikkunneram shift cheyyuka — folic acid 5mg daily start cheyyuka pregnancy
planning nu — OB-GYN Dr. Lekha yodu discuss cheyyuka timing about TSH
stable aayaal mathrame conceive cheyyaavu.

expected_nodes.content: Nisha — TSH 12.5, hypothyroid confirmed
Thyronorm 75mcg morning empty stomach, 30 min before breakfast
Calcium/iron supplements NOT with Thyronorm — 4 hour gap needed
Shift Calcirol to evening (was morning — conflicts with Thyronorm)
TSH must be < 2.5 BEFORE conception — pregnancy planning in 3 months
Repeat TSH in 4 weeks, may need dose increase to 100mcg
Folic acid 5mg daily (pregnancy planning), discuss with OB-GYN Dr. Lekha

title: GENERAL MEDICINE
context: Classic Hyderabadi code-switching — three languages in one note
transcript:
Abbas bhai ka BP control mein nahi aa raha — dawai le raha hai regularly
kehta hai lekin mujhe doubt hai — Telmisartan 40 chal raha tha ab 80 karo
— aur Amlodipine 5mg add karo — ek kaam karo ABPM lagao 24 hour ambulatory
monitoring — white coat hypertension rule out karo — Abbas bhai thoda nervous
type ka aadmi hai clinic mein BP hamesha high aata hai ghar pe normal rehta
hai bol raha hai — to ABPM se pata chal jayega — kidney function check karo
microalbuminuria bhi dekho hypertensive nephropathy early detect karne ke liye —
fundoscopy karwao hypertensive retinopathy grade karo — lipid profile karo
cardiovascular risk assessment karo — agar ABPM mein bhi high aata hai to
triple therapy pe shift karna padega Telmisartan Amlodipine Chlorthalidone —
lifestyle modification pe baat karo — namak kam khao exercise karo weight
kam karo — Abbas bhai ko bolo dawai band mat karo BP normal aane pe bhi.

expected_nodes.content: Telmisartan 40→80mg, add Amlodipine 5mg
24-hour ABPM to rule out white coat hypertension
Renal workup — kidney function + microalbuminuria (hypertensive nephropathy screening)
Fundoscopy for hypertensive retinopathy grading, lipid profile for CV risk
If ABPM confirms → triple therapy (Telmisartan + Amlodipine + Chlorthalidone)
Do NOT stop medication even if BP normalizes (patient education)
Abbas — suspected white coat HTN (clinic BP high, home BP normal, nervous personality)

title: ICU
context: Ventilator weaning trial — complex respiratory management
transcript:
Murugan patient ku ventilator la 5 days aayiduchu — ipo SBT try pannalaam —
settings reduce pannunga PS 5 PEEP 5 FiO2 30% — 30 minutes observe pannunga —
oru hour la RR 35 ku mela poguthu na abort pannidungo — SpO2 90 ku keezh
poguthu na abort — HR 20% increase aana abort — paradoxical breathing iruntha
abort — blood gas pannunga before and after SBT — pH 7.35 ku keezh pogathe
paathukongo — SBT pass aana extubation plan pannalaam — extubation ku munnadi
cuff leak test pannunga — steroids venam na Methylprednisolone 40mg IV
4 hours before extubation — BiPAP ready vachikongo backup ku — reintubation
kit bedside la ready ah irukkanam — dietician protein rich diet start
pannattum muscle wasting prevent panna — family meeting pannunga extubation
plan explain pannunga risks discuss pannunga.

expected_nodes.content: SBT trial — PS 5, PEEP 5, FiO2 30%, observe 30-60 min
Abort SBT if: RR > 35, SpO2 < 90%, HR increase > 20%, paradoxical breathing, pH < 7.35
Pre-extubation — cuff leak test, Methylprednisolone 40mg IV 4hr before
Post-extubation — BiPAP backup ready, reintubation kit bedside
ABG before and after SBT
Protein-rich diet to prevent muscle wasting, family meeting for extubation plan

title: RHEUMATOLOGY
context: Autoimmune patient with multiple comorbidities — polypharmacy review
transcript:
Savitri amma ge rheumatoid arthritis 15 varsha aayithu — ippo flare up
barthidhe joints swollen aagidhe — DAS28 score 5.8 high disease activity —
Methotrexate 15mg weekly continue, folic acid 5mg next day — Hydroxychloroquine
200mg BD continue — but Prednisolone 20mg aagidhe — taper maadbekku slowly
2.5mg prathi 2 weeks ge reduce maadi — Methotrexate dose 20mg ge increase
maadona but aadre LFT check maadi moodlu — yen andre liver enzymes eri
hog barodu — CBC kuda check maadi pancytopenia watch maadbekku — Savitri
amma ge diabetes kuda idhe Metformin 500 BD chal aagidhe — Prednisolone
sugar eththuthhe adke sugar monitoring tight aagi maadbekku — bone density
scan maadbekku long term steroids gi osteoporosis risk idhe — Calcium
Vitamin D start maadi iddre — aur vaccinations update maadi immunosuppression
idhe — flu vaccine pneumococcal vaccine check maadi — COVID booster
aagidheya nodbekku.

expected_nodes.content: Savitri — RA 15 years, flare, DAS28 5.8 (high disease activity)
Continue MTX 15mg weekly → may increase to 20mg (pending LFT), folic acid 5mg next day
Taper Prednisolone 20mg → reduce 2.5mg every 2 weeks
LFT + CBC before MTX dose increase (liver + pancytopenia monitoring)
Prednisolone raises sugar — tighter glucose monitoring needed (has diabetes)
DEXA scan (osteoporosis risk from steroids), start Calcium + Vitamin D
Vaccination update — flu, pneumococcal, COVID booster (immunosuppressed)
